---
title : "Polynomial regression with increasing orders"
author : "Thimoté Dupuch"
date : today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme: zephyr
engine: julia
---


```{julia}
#| echo : false
#| output : false
using Pkg
Pkg.activate("..")
Pkg.status()
```

```{julia}
using CairoMakie, Random, Statistics, LinearAlgebra
```

  
```{julia}
#| output : false
#| echo : false
CairoMakie.set_theme!(theme_latexfonts())
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "svg")
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
colors = Makie.wong_colors()
blue, orange, red = colors[1], colors[2], colors[6]
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
true_function(x) = x.^2 .- 0.5
```
  
```{julia}
#| output : false
function sample_data(n, std_noise, rng)
    Xsample = rand(rng, n) .* 2 .- 1
    Ysample = true_function(Xsample) .+ randn(rng, n) .* std_noise
    return Xsample, Ysample
end
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
function fit_polynomial(X, Y, degree)
    # Build Vandermonde-style design matrix: columns x^0, x^1, ..., x^degree
    Xdata = hcat([X .^ k for k in 0:degree]...)
    w = Xdata \ Y
    return w
end
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
function predict(Xgrid, w)
    degree = length(w) - 1
    Xgriddata = hcat([Xgrid .^ k for k in 0:degree]...)
    return Xgriddata * w
end
```
```{julia}
#| output : false
function train_mse(X, Y, w)
    degree = length(w) - 1
    Xdata = hcat([X .^ k for k in 0:degree]...)
    residuals = Xdata * w .- Y
    return mean(residuals .^ 2)
end
```

```{julia}
function plot_polynomial_regression(; seed=1, n=20, std_noise=0.25, kmax=14)
    rng = MersenneTwister(seed)

    Xgrid = collect(-1:0.001:1)
    Ygrid = true_function(Xgrid)

    Xsample, Ysample = sample_data(n, std_noise, rng)

    nplots = kmax + 1
    ncols = 5
    nrows = ceil(Int, nplots / ncols)

    fig = Figure(size=(ncols * 300, nrows * 300))

    for k in 0:kmax
        row = div(k, ncols) + 1
        col = mod(k, ncols) + 1
        ax = Axis(fig[row, col], limits=((-1, 1), (-1, 1.1)))

        w = fit_polynomial(Xsample, Ysample, k)
        Ygrid_pred = predict(Xgrid, w)

        train_err = train_mse(Xsample, Ysample, w)
        test_err  = std_noise^2 + mean((Ygrid_pred .- Ygrid) .^ 2)

        ax.title =
          "train=$(round(train_err, digits=2)), test=$(round(test_err, digits=2))"

        lines!(ax, Xgrid, Ygrid_pred, color=red,  linewidth=2, label = L"k=%$k")
        lines!(ax, Xgrid, Ygrid,      color=blue, linewidth=2)
        scatter!(ax, Xsample, Ysample, color=:black, marker=:xcross, markersize=10)
        axislegend(ax, position=:rt)
    end

    return fig
end

plot_polynomial_regression()
```


```{julia}
function fit_polynomial_regularized(X, Y, degree; lambda=1e-12)
    Xdata = hcat([X .^ k for k in 0:degree]...)
    # Tikhonov regularisation
    n = size(Xdata, 1)
    w = (Xdata'Xdata + n * lambda * I) \ (Xdata'Y)
    return w, Xdata
end
 
function compute_errors(Xsample, Ysample, Xgrid, Ygrid, kmax, std_noise)
    train_errs = zeros(kmax + 1)
    test_errs  = zeros(kmax + 1)
    for k in 0:kmax
        w, Xdata = fit_polynomial_regularized(Xsample, Ysample, k)
        Xgriddata     = hcat([Xgrid .^ j for j in 0:k]...)
        Ygrid_pred    = Xgriddata * w
        train_errs[k+1] = mean((Xdata * w .- Ysample) .^ 2)
        test_errs[k+1]  = std_noise^2 + mean((Ygrid_pred .- Ygrid) .^ 2)
    end
 
    return train_errs, test_errs
end
 
function run_replications(; seed=1, n=20, std_noise=0.25, kmax=7, nrep=32)
    rng = MersenneTwister(seed)
 
    Xgrid = collect(-1:0.001:1)
    Ygrid = true_function(Xgrid)
 
    train_matrix = zeros(nrep, kmax + 1)
    test_matrix  = zeros(nrep, kmax + 1)
 
    for irep in 1:nrep
        Xsample = rand(rng, n) .* 2 .- 1
        Ysample = true_function(Xsample) .+ randn(rng, n) .* std_noise
 
        train_matrix[irep, :], test_matrix[irep, :] =
            compute_errors(Xsample, Ysample, Xgrid, Ygrid, kmax, std_noise)
    end
 
    return train_matrix, test_matrix
end
 
function plot_errors(; seed=1, n=20, std_noise=0.25, kmax=7, nrep=32)
    train_matrix, test_matrix = run_replications(;
        seed, n, std_noise, kmax, nrep)
 
    ks           = 0:kmax
    mean_train   = mean(train_matrix, dims=1)[:]
    std_train    = std(train_matrix,  dims=1)[:]
    mean_test    = mean(test_matrix,  dims=1)[:]
    std_test     = std(test_matrix,   dims=1)[:]
 
    fig = Figure(size=(600,400))
    ax  = Axis(fig[1, 1];
        xlabel = "Polynomial order k",
        ylabel = "Errors",
        yticks = 0:0.2:1,
        limits = ((0, kmax), (0, 1)),
    )
 
    # Error bands (±1 std)
    band!(ax, ks, mean_test  .- std_test,  mean_test  .+ std_test;  color=(red,  0.2))
    band!(ax, ks, mean_train .- std_train, mean_train .+ std_train; color=(blue, 0.2))
 
    # Mean lines with markers
    scatterlines!(ax, ks, mean_train;
        color=blue, linewidth=2, marker=:xcross, markersize=14, label="Training")
    scatterlines!(ax, ks, mean_test;
        color=red, linewidth=2, marker=:xcross, markersize=14, label="Testing")
 
    # Bayes error
    hlines!(ax, [std_noise^2];
        color=:black, linewidth=1, linestyle=:dash, label="Bayes error")
 
    axislegend(ax, position=:lt)
 
    return fig
end
 
plot_errors()
```
